claim: The three eras of software development are: Tab completion (next-event prediction), agentic coding (agent in driver's seat with human steering), and agent systems (teams of autonomous agents working in cloud)
actionable: Recognize that you're transitioning from single-agent to multi-agent systems; design your workflows to leverage persistent cloud teams rather than one-off local assistants
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Over 60% of code lines in enterprise products are already being changed by AI (as of March 2026), creating challenges around code quality review and managing thousands of lines shipped per day
actionable: When reviewing AI-generated code at scale, implement triage systems and focus on high-risk areas rather than line-by-line review
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Cloud agents at Cursor can run uninterrupted for extended periods (e.g., 1 week), generating millions of lines of code; example: an agent successfully built a functional browser from scratch
actionable: For ambitious long-running projects, use cloud agents that can work asynchronously while you sleep or focus on other tasks; batch complex problems into multi-day agent executions
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Different LLM models excel at different tasks: Cursor models are best for rapid iteration and debugging; Anthropic/OpenAI/Google models are better for high-reasoning tasks like planning
actionable: Implement model delegation: use specialized models for specific phases (planning with GPT-4 level, coding with Cursor models, then verify with another specialized model)
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Cloud agents are self-verifying: they can access their own computers, test changes manually, record videos of execution, and return rich artifacts instead of just diffs
actionable: When deploying agents to production, enable self-verification to reduce manual testing overhead; request video artifacts from agents to confirm changes work before merging
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Cursor integrates with GitHub and enables agents to automatically organize commits by semantic meaning, making code review significantly easier
actionable: Enable GitHub MCPs and Composio integrations in your agent workflows to automate commit organization; this frees reviewers to focus on logic instead of sorting diffs
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Automations (scheduled/event-triggered cloud agents) can eliminate tedious tasks like postmortem writing, feature flag cleanup, and incident response
actionable: Build automations that run nightly to clean up technical debt (feature flags, unused code) or on PagerDuty incident resolved events to draft postmortems with DataDog logs
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Cloud agents can be triggered from Slack via natural language; Cursor detects the relevant repository automatically from Slack context and handles repo selection without explicit instruction
actionable: Integrate your agent with Slack to enable quick feature flag fixes, documentation updates, or bug investigations from chat without leaving your communication platform
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development

claim: Multi-agent team structure with planner agent delegating to worker agents mirrors how human engineering teams are organized, making agent orchestration a natural extension of team practices
actionable: When designing agent workflows, replicate your human team structure: designate a coordinator agent to delegate to specialized worker agents, mirroring feature-branch reviews and merges
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Amrita Venkatraman: 3rd Era of Software Development
